label: near_miss

case_target.description: 当前 case 的主控制对象是 FileService._resolve_safe_path()，它负责把用户输入路径规范化并限制在基目录内。

should_not_report.reason: FileService._resolve_safe_path() fully validates and canonicalizes the filename before read_file() is called. utils/file_utils.py:read_file() only ever receives a pre-validated absolute path from the service layer.
should_not_report.false_positive_risk: A system that inspects utils/file_utils.py in isolation will see open(path) with an externally supplied argument and flag it as an unguarded sink. A system that traces the call from the route layer but fails to enter _resolve_safe_path() will reach read_file() via get_file_content() and still incorrectly flag open() at line 3.

controls.description: os.path.normpath normalizes redundant separators and resolves non-leading '..' components (e.g. a/b/../c → a/c), but preserves leading '..' in relative paths (../../etc/passwd stays as-is). It is not the security boundary; its role is to reduce noise before realpath.
Explicit rejection of absolute paths after normalization, blocking '/etc/passwd'-style inputs.
os.path.realpath resolves symlinks and any remaining '..' components to a canonical absolute path.
Prefix check with os.sep suffix ensures the resolved path is strictly inside BASE_DIR, preventing partial-directory name bypass.
ValueError from _resolve_safe_path() is caught and returns None, preventing any invalid path from reaching read_file().

notes: Difficulty is 'hard' because the guard and sink are in different layers/files. read_file() in utils is intentionally left unguarded — it is a low-level I/O helper that trusts callers to validate paths. The near-miss tests whether the system correctly tracks that only pre-validated paths reach it.
